problem:
Let $A,$ $B,$ $C$ be the angles of a triangle.  Evaluate
\[\begin{vmatrix} \sin^2 A & \cot A & 1 \\ \sin^2 B & \cot B & 1 \\ \sin^2 C & \cot C & 1 \end{vmatrix}.\]

classified_questions.question: What is the determinant of the matrix provided?
classified_questions.label: f

classified_questions.question: How can we expand the determinant using the first column?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: Can we simplify the determinant using the trigonometric identity for cotangent?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: Can we simplify the determinant further using the trigonometric identity for sine?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: How do we use these identities to arrive at the final expression for the determinant?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: Is there any special significance of the value 0 for the final determinant?
classified_questions.label: e

classified_questions.question: Can we use similar techniques to evaluate determinants of matrices with trigonometric functions for different expressions?
classified_questions.label: b
